This alpha incorporates lessons from the first successful migration of a real disposable Codex project.

Changes since alpha.1:

- recognize the renamed ChatGPT Desktop application while excluding the unrelated Codex Computer Use helper from the state-writer check;
- list matching writer processes in refusal errors and fail closed when process inspection itself fails;
- preserve exact project-root path spelling without introducing a trailing slash;
- make `verify` report explicit `verified`, `old_references`, and `destination_threads` summary fields;
- exercise exact-root remapping in the release-binary end-to-end test.

The real validation moved a same-filesystem project directory, migrated four task records and their structured rollout/Desktop references, passed automated verification, and was then confirmed in ChatGPT Desktop. No rollback was required.

This remains a prerelease for synthetic or disposable projects. Close ChatGPT Desktop and state-writing Codex CLI processes before any mutation, review the plan, and retain the backup until recovery is confirmed.
